name: Open Window
desc: Opens an inventory GUI window (chest, crafting table, furnace, enchantment table, etc.) with window ID, type, title, and slot count.

fields.name: slotCount
fields.desc: Number of slots in the window

fields.name: entityId
fields.desc: Entity ID for EntityHorse type (only present for horse GUIs)

notes: Inventory types: minecraft:chest, crafting_table, furnace, dispenser, enchanting_table, brewing_stand, villager, beacon, anvil, hopper, dropper, EntityHorse. RektSky B5 ChestStealer intercepts this packet for silent chest handling — it cancels the vanilla open, stores the window data, and manages the chest entirely via packet-level manipulation.

implementation.overview: S2DPacketOpenWindow opens a container GUI on the client. The server sends the window ID, GUI type (chest/crafting/enchant/etc.), display title, and slot count. ChestStealer modules trigger on this packet to start auto-stealing. RektSky B5 ChestStealer intercepts S2D to silently track chest data without displaying the GUI. S31PacketWindowProperty follows for property-driven GUIs (enchantment levels, brewing progress).

modules.name: ChestStealer Silent Mode (RektSky B5)
modules.purpose: Intercept S2DPacketOpenWindow to track chest data silently — the GUI is never shown to the player, but the chest items are read and stolen via packet-level manipulation. The window title is used to verify it's a chest.
modules.how_it_works: When S2D arrives with type 'minecraft:chest', the module cancels the vanilla handler (setCanceled(true)) and stores the windowId and slotCount. It creates a fake ContainerChest locally (not opened to the screen) and tracks it via S30PacketWindowItems. Items are stolen by constructing C0EPacketClickWindow directly against this silent container.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleOpenWindow(S2DPacketOpenWindow) — opens a GuiChest/GuiCrafting/etc. on the client. If cancelled, no GUI is shown.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Silent chest stealing (cancelling S2D and handling all inventory via direct packets) is not detectable by anti-cheats because the server doesn't know whether the client showed the GUI. The server just sends S2D, waits for C0E clicks, and sends S2D close. However, some anti-cheats check that C0E clicks from a 'chest' are preceded by an S2D open — if S2D was cancelled and the server didn't process a legitimate open, the C0E may still work.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleOpenWindow(S2DPacketOpenWindow) — creates the GUI and opens it. RektSky B5 ChestStealer cancels to prevent GUI display.

Server: Sent when the player right-clicks a container block (chest, furnace, crafting table, etc.).
implementation.client_variations:
RektSky B5: ChestStealer cancels S2D for silent mode, stores windowId for direct C0E packet manipulation.
Rise 6.0: ChestStealer/AutoArmor detect S2D to trigger inventory management.
Sigma 4.11: AutoArmor detects S2D 'minecraft:enchanting_table' for enchant detection.
Tenacity 6.0: ChestStealer triggers on S2D.
Jigsaw 0.26: No specific S2D module.
Aqua 13: EventPacket fires; no specific S2D handler.
